html:
Exclusive to:  

The Alchemy Engine is the tier 2 Science station. It requires a Science Machine to prototype for all characters except Wickerbottom, and costs 4 Boards, 2 Cut Stones, and 6 Gold Nuggets to craft. Prototyping an Item using the Alchemy Engine will increase Sanity by 15.

See also: Crafting

Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants, Shipwrecked, and Hamlet DLCs, as well as Don't Starve Together, the 6 Gold Nuggets in the crafting recipe are replaced by 2 Electrical Doodads.

In the Shipwrecked DLC, the Alchemy Engine can be flooded.

Tips

  • An easy way to gather the crafting materials for an Alchemy Engine is to hammer down Pig Houses for Boards and Cut Stones, and to mine Gold Vein Boulders for Gold.
  • The Alchemy Engine is able to unlock Tier 1 items without the need for a nearby Science Machine, so it may be beneficial to break down the Science Machine for resources after building it.
  • Since Wickerbottom has Tier 2 items unlocked with the Science Machine, this machine is only necessary for crafting her book On Tentacles.

Trivia

  • In the early Beta version of Don't Starve, the game used a "Research Points" system, instead of prototyping. Crafting this station cost 200 Research Points.
  • As of the Hungry For Your Hunger update, Tier 3 recipes crafted using the Shadow Manipulator have been moved to the Alchemy Engine, due to the separation of Science and Magic.

Exclusive to:
“Even more scienc-y than the last one!”
–Wilson
“Even I don't know everything, yet.”
–Willow
“I am not sure how I feel about... science.”
–Wolfgang
“I will learn unspeakable things.”
–Wendy
“HELLO, FRIEND”
–WX-78
“It's a research station. I can learn new things with it.”
–Wickerbottom
“This is getting strange.”
–Woodie
“It unlocks recipes of middling utility.”
–Maxwell
“Even alchemy operates differently here.”
–Wagstaff
“Perhaps science can enhance my cömbat skills.”
–Wigfrid
“Father used to work on something like that.”
–Webber
“I wish you were a do-all-the-work-for-me machine.”
–Walani
“Oh, the things I'll learn!”
–Warly
“It does whut?”
–Woodlegs
“WILBA AM HATH BIG'TH BRAIN”
–Wilba
“More things to make!”
–Wormwood
“Alright machine, tell me something I don't know.”
–Wheeler
“I guess proximity activates the whirlygigs?”
–Winona
“Oh, how amusing!”
–Wortox
“All-kemmy... en... jun...”
–Wurt
“They never taught us about alchemy in school.”
–Walter
“It turns out that science is a lot easier than I thought!”
–Wanda
The Alchemy Engine is the tier 2 Science station. It requires a Science Machine to prototype for all characters except Wickerbottom, and costs 4 Boards, 2 Cut Stones, and 6 Gold Nuggets to craft. Prototyping an Item using the Alchemy Engine will increase Sanity by 15.

Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Reign of Giants, Shipwrecked, and Hamlet DLCs, as well as Don't Starve Together, the 6 Gold Nuggets in the crafting recipe are replaced by 2 Electrical Doodads.
In the Shipwrecked DLC, the Alchemy Engine can be flooded.
Tips[edit | edit source]
An easy way to gather the crafting materials for an Alchemy Engine is to hammer down Pig Houses for Boards and Cut Stones, and to mine Gold Vein Boulders for Gold.
The Alchemy Engine is able to unlock Tier 1 items without the need for a nearby Science Machine, so it may be beneficial to break down the Science Machine for resources after building it.
Since Wickerbottom has Tier 2 items unlocked with the Science Machine, this machine is only necessary for crafting her book On Tentacles.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
In the early Beta version of Don't Starve, the game used a "Research Points" system, instead of prototyping. Crafting this station cost 200 Research Points.
As of the Hungry For Your Hunger update, Tier 3 recipes crafted using the Shadow Manipulator have been moved to the Alchemy Engine, due to the separation of Science and Magic.
